prompt: READ-ONLY RESEARCH + DESIGN. Produce a split spec for AngelEye → 'AngelSentinel'. Do NOT modify any AngelEye CODE (read-only); the ONLY write is the spec document. STEPS: (1) UNDERSTAND THE SENTINEL PATTERN: read ~/dev/ad/apps/appysentinal (its README/docs/.mochaccino/code) AND the concept in memory 'appystack-vs-sentinel' / 'watchtower-sentinel-bus-direction'. Characterise what a 'Sentinel' is: always-on observer/collector, ingests events, never runs claude itself, pub/sub + replay. (2) IDENTIFY ALL OF ANGELEYE'S CODE AND WHERE IT LIVES: map ~/dev/ad/apps/angeleye (server/, client/, shared/, scripts/, etc.). Split every part into the TWO PRODUCTS: (a) VISUALISATION CONTROL PLANE = the dashboard/UI (client, the viz/query API) — stays 'AngelEye'; (b) THE SENTINEL = always-on, absorbs the hooks: session reading + hook ingestion endpoints + collectors + the liveness API. List concrete files/dirs under each. Cite paths. (3) DRAFT THE SPLIT SPEC for 'AngelSentinel' (the Sentinel half): what code moves to AngelSentinel vs stays in AngelEye Control Plane; the always-on / daemonized design (it needs a real production-start command + service — it currently only has a dev launcher, no production serve); how the two halves communicate; AND the new TOOL-USAGE TELEMETRY capability — AngelSentinel already ingests tool-calls + skill-calls via hooks, so spec a feature that tracks which skills/workflows/commands actually get used (counts, last-used) to feed self-evolution (optimise heavy, deprecate dead). Note the 'could-use-a-big-refactor' areas you find. (4) WRITE THE SPEC to ~/dev/ad/apps/angeleye/docs/requirements/angelsentinel-split-spec-2026-06-08.md (this is a requirement doc for Ralphy to build later — app owns its features; do NOT implement, just spec). Tag claims verified(path)/inference/unknown. (5) Report ONE line: where the spec landed + the headline split (X files → Sentinel, Y → Control Plane). Then complete run-next-workflow bookkeeping (run record + move to done/ + reports/ handback).
